meaning: Always wanting to know about other people's private business; too curious about things that don't concern you.
example: She's so nosy — she literally asked how much I paid for my apartment.

meaning: Always telling other people what to do in an annoying way; acting like you're in charge when you're not.
example: Stop being so bossy. It's a group project, not your project.

meaning: Too aggressive in trying to get what you want; forcing your opinions or products on others.
example: The salesman was so pushy, I just walked out of the store.

meaning: Constantly wanting attention, reassurance, or affection from others; emotionally dependent.
example: He texts me like 20 times a day. It's getting really needy.

meaning: Unwilling to let go of someone; emotionally attached to the point of being suffocating.
example: She got really clingy after like two dates. I had to take a step back.

meaning: Having frequent and unpredictable changes in mood; hard to read emotionally.
example: Don't take it personally — he's just moody in the mornings.

meaning: Irritable and bad-tempered, often because of tiredness or discomfort.
example: I'm so cranky right now. I haven't had coffee yet.

meaning: Sullen and unfriendly; in a persistently bad mood, like a grumbling old man.
example: My dad gets so grumpy when the team loses.

meaning: Acting superior to others; thinking you're better than everyone else in a snobbish way.
example: The staff at that restaurant were so snooty. Never going back.

meaning: Looking down on people who are seen as inferior; elitist and dismissive.
example: He got really snobby after he moved to that fancy neighborhood.

meaning: Curt and rude in speech; responding sharply as if annoyed.
example: Why are you being so snippy? I was just asking a question.

meaning: Unreliable; likely to cancel plans or not follow through on commitments.
example: Don't count on him — he's super flaky. He'll probably cancel last minute.

meaning: Dishonest or suspicious; giving the impression that something illegal or unethical is going on.
example: Something about that deal feels really shady. I wouldn't sign anything.

meaning: Seeming dishonest, unsafe, or of questionable character; slightly shady.
example: That neighborhood gets pretty sketchy after midnight.

meaning: Causing an unpleasant feeling of unease or disgust; making your skin crawl.
example: This guy at the bar kept staring at me. So creepy.

meaning: Overly confident in a way that annoys others; arrogantly self-assured.
example: He just got promoted and now he's acting all cocky about it.

meaning: Brave and determined; willing to take risks even when it's scary.
example: It was gutsy of her to quit her job and start her own thing.

meaning: Easily offended or upset; overly sensitive about particular topics.
example: Don't bring up his ex — he's really touchy about it.

meaning: Lively, determined, and not afraid of an argument; spirited and spunky.
example: She's feisty — she argued with the manager and actually got a refund.

meaning: Fond of talking a lot; friendly and talkative in a casual way.
example: My Uber driver was so chatty. Talked the whole way there.

meaning: Boldly rude or impudent; having a lot of nerve to do something cheeky.
example: It was pretty nervy of him to ask for a raise after being late every day.

meaning: Unwilling to spend money or share things; extremely mean with money.
example: He never tips. Like, ever. So stingy.

meaning: Weak and lacking in courage or determination; spineless.
example: Don't be wimpy about it — just tell her how you feel.

meaning: Anxious and easily startled; on edge and nervous.
example: The loud music kept making her jumpy all night.

meaning: Restless and unable to stay still; impatient and fidgety.
example: I've been sitting in this waiting room for an hour. I'm getting antsy.

meaning: Complaining in a feeble or irritating way; always grumbling without doing anything about it.
example: He was so whiny about the food. Just eat it or don't.

meaning: Behaving as if you are more important or knowledgeable than you really are; self-important and arrogant.
example: He gave this whole pompous speech about wine like he's some kind of expert.

meaning: Entering a situation where you're not wanted; violating someone's personal space or privacy.
example: The questions got really intrusive — like, why do you need to know that?

meaning: Able to understand and share the feelings of another person; genuinely caring about others' emotions.
example: She's so empathetic — she always knows exactly what to say when you're going through something.

meaning: Comfortable with and good at home life — cooking, cleaning, taking care of things around the house.
example: He's surprisingly domesticated — makes dinner every night and keeps the place spotless.

meaning: Going against widely accepted beliefs or norms; seen as shocking or unacceptable to the mainstream.
example: I know it's heretical, but I actually think the sequel is better than the original.

meaning: Expressing hostility or resentment indirectly rather than openly; sulking or giving the silent treatment instead of speaking up.
example: He didn't say anything but just slammed every door. So passive-aggressive.

meaning: Talking down to people as if they're less intelligent or capable than you; patronizing.
example: The way she explained it was so condescending — like we were five years old.

meaning: Convinced you are morally superior to others; sanctimonious and preachy about your own virtue.
example: I agree with what he's saying but he's so self-righteous about it.

meaning: Domineering and impossible to ignore; trying to control everyone around you.
example: Her mom is so overbearing — she calls like three times a day.

meaning: Very nervous and easily stressed; wound up too tight emotionally.
example: She's a little high-strung — just give her a heads-up before any changes.

meaning: Making subtle, mean-spirited comments about others; bitchy in a sneaky way.
example: The comments in that group chat were getting really catty.

meaning: Positive and cheerful; having an optimistic, energetic attitude.
example: She was surprisingly upbeat given everything she's dealing with.

meaning: Noticeably happy and in good spirits; radiating warmth and positivity.
example: He's always cheerful at work, even on Monday mornings.

meaning: Full of high spirits and fun; jovially happy — think Santa Claus energy.
example: Everyone was jolly by the end of the night. Good party.

meaning: Lively and cheerful in an energetic way; perkily upbeat.
example: You're very chipper this morning — did something good happen?

meaning: Excitedly happy to the point of feeling light-headed; giggly and elated.
example: She was giddy all day after he texted her back.

meaning: Hanging or falling downward in a tired or sad way; lacking energy or brightness.
example: He walked in looking all droopy — clearly did not sleep last night.

meaning: Slightly drunk; feeling the pleasant early effects of alcohol without being wasted.
example: Two glasses in and I'm already tipsy. I'm such a lightweight.

meaning: Pleasantly affected by alcohol; a step past tipsy but still functional.
example: I'm not drunk, just buzzed. Big difference.

meaning: Very drunk; well past the point of being in control.
example: He was absolutely hammered by 9pm. It was embarrassing.

meaning: Extremely drunk; completely out of it from alcohol.
example: I don't remember anything after midnight — I was wasted.

meaning: Pleasantly firm and crunchy on the outside, especially when fried or baked well.
example: The skin on that chicken was perfectly crispy. So good.

meaning: Making a loud crunching sound when bitten; firm enough to crunch.
example: I like my peanut butter crunchy, not smooth.

meaning: Soft, sticky, and wet in a pleasantly indulgent way — think melted cheese or brownie centers.
example: That brownie was so gooey in the middle. Insane.

meaning: Needing a lot of chewing; not tender. Can be positive (bagel) or negative (tough steak).
example: These cookies are chewy and soft — exactly how I like them.

meaning: Soft and wet from soaking up too much liquid; unpleasantly waterlogged.
example: I hate soggy fries. Just ruin the whole meal.

meaning: Soft and pulpy to the point of being unpleasant; overcooked or over-ripe.
example: The pasta was way overcooked — totally mushy.

meaning: Covered in or having too much oil or fat; unpleasantly oily.
example: I love greasy diner food when I'm hungover.

meaning: Tasting of salt. In slang: bitter, resentful, or annoyed about something.
example: He's still salty about losing the game last week.

meaning: Having a strong, hot flavor from chili or spices. Also informally: edgy or provocative.
example: That tweet was spicy. She's not holding back.

meaning: Having a sharp, refreshing flavor, often from citrus. Also: energetic and lively.
example: This lemon dressing is so zesty — I need the recipe.

meaning: Having a strong, sharp, slightly sour taste that tingles the tongue.
example: The sauce is a little tangy — kind of like a vinegar BBQ thing.

meaning: Smelling or tasting of fish. In everyday use: suspicious or dishonest.
example: His story doesn't add up. Something smells fishy.

meaning: Tasting or smelling of nuts. Informally: slightly crazy or eccentric.
example: This cheese has a really nutty flavor — in a good way.

meaning: Tasting rich and smooth like butter; also used for a very smooth texture.
example: The chardonnay was super buttery. A bit too much for me.

meaning: Containing a lot of alcohol; tasting strongly of alcohol. Also describes a heavy drinker.
example: These cocktails are really boozy — only have one if you're driving.

meaning: Having a pleasant, sweet smell; aromatic and inviting to the nose.
example: The bread just came out of the oven and it's so fragrant in here.

meaning: Yielding easily to touch; cooked or prepared so that it melts in the mouth.
example: The short rib was fall-apart soft and tender. Cooked it for six hours.

meaning: Having a rough, scratchy quality — usually describing a voice.
example: She had this really raspy voice, like she'd been singing all night. Super cool actually.

meaning: Unpleasantly warm and humid; the kind of weather that makes you sweat just standing still.
example: Tokyo in August is so muggy. I can't handle it.

meaning: Noticeably cold but not freezing; cool enough to make you want a jacket.
example: It's a bit chilly out — maybe grab a jacket.

meaning: Pleasantly windy. Also describes someone who is casual and relaxed.
example: It's breezy out but sunny — perfect for a walk.

meaning: Full of fog and hard to see through. Also: mentally unclear.
example: Sorry, I'm still a bit foggy — I just woke up.

meaning: Covered with a layer of dust; not used or cleaned in a long time.
example: This room is so dusty. When did we last open it?

meaning: Covered in rust. Figuratively: out of practice and not as sharp as you used to be.
example: My French is a bit rusty — I haven't spoken it in years.

meaning: Having a stale, damp smell from being closed up for too long; smelling old.
example: The cabin smelled kind of musty, but it was fine once we opened the windows.

meaning: Lacking fresh air. Also describes someone who is overly formal and conservative.
example: It's so stuffy in here. Can we open a window?

meaning: Letting in unpleasant cold air through gaps; said of rooms with poor insulation.
example: Our old apartment was so drafty in winter. Froze every night.

meaning: Having many bumps or rough patches; not smooth.
example: That was a really bumpy flight. I spilled my drink twice.

meaning: Full of irregular lumps; not evenly smooth.
example: The mattress was so lumpy I could barely sleep.

meaning: Full of short, irregular waves — used for water or sea conditions. Also: abrupt and disjointed.
example: The crossing was really choppy — half the boat was seasick.

meaning: Poorly made and likely to collapse; unsteady and in bad condition.
example: I don't trust that rickety old chair — it's gonna break.

meaning: Making a high-pitched sound when touched or moved.
example: The floor is so squeaky — I can't sneak to the kitchen at night.

meaning: Allowing water or other fluid to escape through a hole or crack.
example: The faucet's been leaky for weeks. We need to call someone.

meaning: Tending to stick to things; covered in a substance that sticks.
example: The kids got into the syrup — everything is sticky now.

meaning: Difficult to hold or walk on because of being smooth or wet.
example: Watch out — the floor is slippery after they mopped.

meaning: Weak and thin; not strong enough to do the job properly.
example: That excuse is pretty flimsy. Nobody's buying it.

meaning: Large and taking up a lot of space; awkward to carry because of its size.
example: The new couch is great but it's so bulky we can barely move around.

meaning: Overwhelmed with too much work; completely buried and unable to keep up.
example: Can we push the meeting? I'm totally swamped this week.

meaning: Full of frantic activity; chaotically busy with no breathing room.
example: It's been a hectic week — back-to-back meetings every single day.

meaning: Inconsistent; good in some places but not others.
example: The Wi-Fi is really spotty on this floor.

meaning: Uneven; occurring in isolated areas rather than uniformly.
example: My Japanese is a bit patchy — fine for business but I struggle with slang.

meaning: Richly green and full of growth; also luxuriously abundant or sensually rich.
example: The garden was so lush after all the rain.

meaning: Dirty and dull-looking; shabby and gloomy in appearance.
example: The hallway was really dingy — bad lighting, peeling paint.

meaning: Filled with too many things in a disorganized way; messy with stuff everywhere.
example: My desk is so cluttered I can't find anything.

meaning: Thinly spread; not dense or crowded — can describe furniture, hair, population, etc.
example: The apartment was really sparse — just a mattress and a lamp.

meaning: Slightly overweight in a soft, round way — often used affectionately.
example: Look at those chubby little baby hands. I can't.

meaning: Short and slightly fat, especially in the fingers or limbs; chubbier and more child-like in feel.
example: He's got the cutest pudgy fingers.

meaning: Tall and thin in an awkward way; ungracefully slim with long limbs.
example: He shot up over the summer — totally lanky now.

meaning: So thin that your bones are visible; very skinny.
example: You can tell she hasn't been eating well — she looks bony.

meaning: Untidy and slightly dirty in appearance; messy-looking.
example: He showed up to the interview looking all scruffy. Not a good look.

meaning: Careless and untidy; lacking attention to detail or effort.
example: The report was really sloppy — typos everywhere. Send it back.

meaning: Dressed in clothes that are dull and unfashionable; dowdy.
example: That outfit makes her look frumpy — she can do so much better.

meaning: Elaborate and expensive-looking; more decorative or upscale than usual.
example: Oh, we're getting fancy tonight — are you buying?

meaning: Extravagantly bright or showy in a way that is tasteless or vulgar.
example: The decorations were so gaudy — like, rhinestones everywhere.

meaning: Showing poor taste; cheap and vulgar-looking.
example: Charging guests a cover fee at your own birthday party is so tacky.

meaning: In a poor condition from age or overuse; worn out and neglected-looking.
example: The hotel looked nice in photos but it was pretty shabby in person.

meaning: Stylish and attractive in a flashy way; looking impressively neat or sharp.
example: Nice jacket — very snazzy.

meaning: Stylish and sophisticated; showing high quality and good taste.
example: She handled the whole situation really classy. Impressed.

meaning: Large, solid, and muscular; impressively substantial in size or build.
example: That was a beefy book — 600 pages. But worth it.

meaning: More expensive than expected or desired; on the costly side.
example: The food was great but a little pricey for a weeknight dinner.

meaning: Unpleasant, disgusting, or mean. Can also mean impressively good in slang.
example: That was a nasty fall — you okay?

meaning: Aspiring to or enjoying an upper-class lifestyle; luxurious in taste, sometimes pretentiously so.
example: She only stays at five-star hotels now. She's gotten so boujee.

meaning: Strongly built and weather-beaten in an attractive, masculine way; tough-looking.
example: He's got that rugged outdoorsy thing going on. Very Pacific Northwest.

meaning: Neat and stylish in appearance, especially for a man; smartly dressed.
example: He showed up in a suit looking really dapper. Old school but it worked.

meaning: With hair or clothes untidy and disarranged; looking like you just rolled out of bed.
example: She ran in looking completely disheveled — clearly had a rough morning.

meaning: Lean and haggard, especially from illness or overwork; unhealthily thin.
example: He looked gaunt after the hospital stay. Really worried about him.

meaning: Not immediately obvious; delicate and understated rather than loud or direct.
example: The design change is subtle but it makes a big difference.

meaning: Lacking taste or sophistication; embarrassingly sentimental or predictable.
example: I know it's cheesy, but I actually love that song.

meaning: Old-fashioned and overly sentimental; trying too hard to be funny in a tired, clichéd way.
example: His jokes are so corny but honestly I'm here for it.

meaning: Notably happy and optimistic; bright and pleasant in manner.
example: She's always so cheery in the morning. How does she do it?

meaning: Clever and funny, especially in a quick, sharp way.
example: His captions are so witty — every single one.

meaning: Enthusiastically interested in academic or technical subjects in a way seen as socially awkward.
example: I know this is nerdy, but I actually read the whole manual.

meaning: Socially awkward and unfashionable but in an endearing, harmless way.
example: He's a little dorky but that's kind of why I like him.

meaning: Silly and harmlessly stupid in a lovable way; playfully absurd.
example: He made this whole goofy dance to celebrate. So him.

meaning: Having unusual and interesting traits; charmingly unconventional.
example: Her style is quirky but she totally pulls it off.

meaning: Giving moral lectures in an irritating, self-righteous way.
example: I agree with his point but he gets so preachy about it.

meaning: Hard to please; overly particular about details.
example: He's so fussy about coffee — it has to be exactly 65 degrees.

meaning: Quick and efficient; also stylish. Can also mean irritably sharp in speech.
example: Love the new logo — clean and snappy.

meaning: Having practical knowledge and good judgment in a particular area.
example: She's really media-savvy. Knows exactly what gets engagement.

meaning: Short, direct, and full of impact; effective because of its brevity and energy.
example: The tagline is punchy — it sticks with you.

meaning: Only concerned with surface appearances; lacking depth or genuine understanding.
example: The conversation was pretty superficial — nobody said anything real.

meaning: Following what is traditionally done; ordinary and unoriginal.
example: It's a bit conventional for my taste, but it'll work for the client.

meaning: Very direct and honest, sometimes to the point of being rude; not softening the truth.
example: She was blunt about it — told me the pitch wasn't working. I appreciated it.

meaning: Rudely brief in speech; answering with as few words as possible in a cold way.
example: His reply was really curt. Just 'noted.' Nothing else.

meaning: Using more words than necessary; long-winded and hard to get to the point.
example: The brief was so verbose — took me 20 minutes to figure out what they actually wanted.

meaning: Not clear or specific; deliberately or accidentally hard to pin down.
example: The feedback was really vague — 'make it pop' doesn't mean anything.

meaning: Mysterious and hard to understand; deliberately obscure or puzzling.
example: He sent this really cryptic message and then went offline. What does that mean?

meaning: Expressing ideas fluently and persuasively; beautifully articulate.
example: Her closing statement was so eloquent — the whole room went quiet.

meaning: Able to express thoughts clearly and effectively in words.
example: He's really articulate for someone who just started in the role.

meaning: Impossible to understand; rambling and making no logical sense.
example: By the end of the night he was completely incoherent.

meaning: Quietly or secretly; used to admit something without making a big deal of it.
example: I lowkey love that show. Don't tell anyone.

meaning: Openly and obviously; the opposite of lowkey — no shame about it.
example: I am highkey obsessed with this album right now.

meaning: Over the top; doing way too much for the situation.
example: She showed up in a ballgown to a casual dinner. So extra.

meaning: Unoriginal and predictable in taste; following mainstream trends without any individual style.
example: Pumpkin spice latte? That's so basic." (Note: often said affectionately)

meaning: Mediocre; neither good nor bad — just disappointingly average.
example: The movie was mid. I wouldn't watch it again but it wasn't terrible.

meaning: Suspicious; something feels off or not trustworthy. Short for 'suspicious.'
example: The way he keeps changing the story is really sus.

meaning: Deeply shaken or shocked; emotionally rattled by something unexpected.
example: I'm still shook — did not see that ending coming.

meaning: Visibly bothered or upset about something; trying too hard to seem unbothered but failing.
example: Why is he so pressed about a comment? Let it go.

meaning: Completely exhausted and drained from prolonged stress or overwork; past the point of tired.
example: I'm so burnt out. I haven't had a day off in three weeks.

meaning: Willing and ready; on board with something. Short for 'I'm down for it.'
example: Tacos tonight? I'm down.
